input.thought: Open Gladly official site and developer/help pages to verify API, auth, and access details.

arguments.task: Research the app Gladly using official first-party pages only where possible. Open and inspect pages on gladly.com for developer docs, API/authentication docs, pricing/access information, support/help center articles, changelog/product update pages, and any references to MCP or GitHub. Also use search engine queries in the browser to find official Gladly API docs if navigation is insufficient. Extract exact URLs and concise findings for: one-line product description, whether public API exists, API style (REST/GraphQL), authentication methods, any rate limit notes, whether access appears self-serve or requires sales/contact/admin approval, and whether an MCP server exists on official or GitHub pages. Return only findings supported by visited pages, quoting key wording where useful.

raw_result.description: Customer service platform with AI and omnichannel support tools for customer experience teams.
raw_result.auth_notes: Gladly developer docs expose a REST API with an Authentication section and help-center evidence indicates API tokens are generated in Gladly under Settings > App Developer Tools; exact auth header format and whether any additional auth modes exist were not fully verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Evidence indicates API access is available to existing Gladly customers from the product UI rather than an open public self-serve developer signup; token generation appears to require in-product access and likely an admin/API-user role.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST developer documentation exists, including authentication and rate-limit sections, suggesting a meaningful API surface for Gladly platform operations; breadth beyond those sections was not exhaustively verified from the gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server evidence was found in the gathered Gladly or GitHub searches; absence is not definitive.

raw_result.blocker: Requires an existing Gladly customer account and likely admin-level in-product setup to generate API tokens; self-serve access was not verified.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence from the REST rate-limit section indicates a default limit of 10 requests/second for standard REST methods and 10 requests/minute with 2 concurrent requests for the Reports API.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer site exists at developer.gladly.com with REST docs, auth section, and rate-limit section. Help-center evidence suggests API tokens are created from Settings > App Developer Tools, indicating customer-managed token setup inside the product.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Access tier and auth specifics are inferred from gathered browser evidence rather than directly quoted from a dedicated pricing/access page; MCP absence is also non-definitive without broader verification.
